title: pachycladon latesiliquum
intro: Pachycladon latesiliquum, also known as the New Zealand spinach or Poro poro, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pachycladon latesiliquum are said to include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as potentially aiding in the treatment of certain respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often consumed as a tea or used in infusions, and they can also be dried and used in various forms of traditional medicine.
